title: erica anguligera
intro: Erica anguligera, also known as the angled heather, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountains of southern Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly as an astringent and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestive issues. Erica anguligera can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
